response: Chancellor Otto von Bismarck and the Reichstadt Agreement were also part of the **Dreikaiserabkommen** (League of the Three Emperors), a secret alliance between Germany, Russia, and Austria-Hungary established in 1873. This alliance, which Bismarck led, aimed to control Eastern Europe and prevent German aggression by balancing power among the three nations. The Reichstadt Agreement, signed in 1876, was a key component of this alliance, reflecting the shared goals of the three emperors to address the unrest in the Ottoman Empire and secure influence in the Balkans.
